fields.indication_summary: Progestin indicated for use by females of reproductive potential to prevent pregnancy. Limitations of Use: not recommended as a long-term (longer than 2 years) birth control method unless other options are considered inadequate.
fields.dosage_forms_strengths: Vials containing sterile aqueous suspension: 150 mg per mL; prefilled syringes packaged with 22-gauge x 1 1/2 inch needles

notes: SPL title says 'DEPO-PROVERA' but the label content consistently uses 'DEPO-PROVERA CI' (CI = Contraceptive Injection) -- the CI suffix distinguishes this contraceptive product from the oncology-dose Depo-Provera, so brand matching needs to tolerate both forms. All revision dates agree on December 2025: PI 'Revised: 12/2025', patient labeling 'Revised December 2025' (FDA-approved patient labeling, not a formal Medication Guide, hence medication_guide null), SPL footer 12/2025. Initial U.S. Approval 1959 refers to the medroxyprogesterone acetate molecule; this contraceptive NDA020246's own marketing start in the SPL data is 10/29/1992 -- two different, both-real years, do not conflate. Two package NDCs (vial 0009-0746-30, prefilled syringe 0009-7376-11); ndc left null rather than picking one.
